## 4. Energy / Materials
**Uranium spot price decline:** Despite strong policy signals, uranium spot price declined from $101.50/lb in January 2026 to approximately $85.90/lb by mid-March. | MEDIUM | CONTRADICTS

**March 2026 Nuclear Energy Summit:** The March 2026 Nuclear Energy Summit formalized commitments to triple global nuclear capacity by 2050, establishing one of the clearest long-term demand signals the uranium market has seen in decades. | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS

**AI-driven uranium demand:** AI and data centers are emerging as major drivers of nuclear demand, with 85% of surveyed investors anticipating uranium prices in the $100-$120/lb range for 2026, with upside scenarios to $135/lb if supply fails to respond. | HIGH | CONFIRMS

**Supply tightening:** Global uranium mine production is falling short of reactor requirements, with a supply deficit expected to build over the next decade. Near-term supply is hindered by long lead times and high capital intensity. | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS

**LLM notes:** The uranium sector faces a near-term price contradiction — spot prices down ~15% from January peak despite bullish long-term fundamentals (March Summit, AI power demand, supply deficits). This is a classic signal-lag pattern: policy signals today translate to fuel contracting 3-10 years out (per index 1-11), not immediate spot demand. Strategic_materials sleeve (URNM, REMX, COPX) has top momentum ranks (1.0, 0.89, 0.89) — momentum already reflects the medium/long-term thesis. Near-term price weakness is priced. HIGH materiality rating reflects structural demand shift from AI infrastructure, which is a new fundamental driver not fully captured in historical momentum models.
